members.name: GenerateTriangleMesh
members.summary: Returns a [TriangleMesh] with the sprite's vertices following its current configuration (such as its [member axis] and [member pixel_size]).

members.name: GetDrawFlag
members.summary: Returns the value of the specified flag.

members.name: GetItemRect
members.summary: Returns the rectangle representing this sprite.

members.name: SetDrawFlag
members.signature: public void SetDrawFlag(int flag, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the specified flag will be enabled.

members.name: AlphaAntialiasingEdge
members.summary: Threshold at which antialiasing will be applied on the alpha channel.

members.name: AlphaAntialiasingMode
members.summary: The type of alpha antialiasing to apply.

members.name: AlphaCut
members.summary: The alpha cutting mode to use for the sprite.

members.name: AlphaHashScale
members.summary: The hashing scale for Alpha Hash. Recommended values between [code]0[/code] and [code]2[/code].

members.name: AlphaScissorThreshold
members.summary: Threshold at which the alpha scissor will discard values.

members.name: Axis
members.summary: The direction in which the front of the texture faces.

members.name: Billboard
members.summary: The billboard mode to use for the sprite. [b]Note:[/b] When billboarding is enabled and the material also casts shadows, billboards will face [b]the[/b] camera in the scene when rendering shadows. In scenes with multiple cameras, the intended shadow cannot be determined and this will result in undefined behavior. See [url=https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/72638]GitHub Pull Request #72638[/url] for details.

members.name: Centered
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], texture will be centered.

members.name: DoubleSided
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], texture can be seen from the back as well, if [code]false[/code], it is invisible when looking at it from behind.

members.name: FixedSize
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the texture is rendered at the same size regardless of distance. The texture's size on screen is the same as if the camera was [code]1.0[/code] units away from the texture's origin, regardless of the actual distance from the camera. The [Camera3D]'s field of view (or [member Camera3D.size] when in orthogonal/frustum mode) still affects the size the sprite is drawn at.

members.name: FlipH
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], texture is flipped horizontally.

members.name: FlipV
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], texture is flipped vertically.

members.name: Modulate
members.summary: A color value used to [i]multiply[/i] the texture's colors. Can be used for mood-coloring or to simulate the color of ambient light. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike [member CanvasItem.modulate] for 2D, colors with values above [code]1.0[/code] (overbright) are not supported. [b]Note:[/b] If a [member GeometryInstance3D.material_override] is defined on the [SpriteBase3D], the material override must be configured to take vertex colors into account for albedo. Otherwise, the color defined in [member modulate] will be ignored. For a [BaseMaterial3D], [member BaseMaterial3D.vertex_color_use_as_albedo] must be [code]true[/code]. For a [ShaderMaterial], [code]ALBEDO *= COLOR.rgb;[/code] must be inserted in the shader's [code]fragment()[/code] function.

members.name: NoDepthTest
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], depth testing is disabled and the object will be drawn in render order.

members.name: Offset
members.summary: The texture's drawing offset. [b]Note:[/b] When you increase [member offset].y in Sprite3D, the sprite moves upward in world space (i.e., +Y is up).

members.name: PixelSize
members.summary: The size of one pixel's width on the sprite to scale it in 3D.

members.name: RenderPriority
members.summary: Sets the render priority for the sprite. Higher priority objects will be sorted in front of lower priority objects. [b]Note:[/b] This only applies if [member alpha_cut] is set to [constant ALPHA_CUT_DISABLED] (default value). [b]Note:[/b] This only applies to sorting of transparent objects. This will not impact how transparent objects are sorted relative to opaque objects. This is because opaque objects are not sorted, while transparent objects are sorted from back to front (subject to priority).

members.name: Shaded
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [Light3D] in the [Environment] has effects on the sprite.

members.name: TextureFilter
members.summary: Filter flags for the texture. [b]Note:[/b] Linear filtering may cause artifacts around the edges, which are especially noticeable on opaque textures. To prevent this, use textures with transparent or identical colors around the edges.

members.name: Transparent
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the texture's transparency and the opacity are used to make those parts of the sprite invisible.

values.name: ALPHA_CUT_DISABLED
values.summary: This mode performs standard alpha blending. It can display translucent areas, but transparency sorting issues may be visible when multiple transparent materials are overlapping.

values.name: ALPHA_CUT_DISCARD
values.summary: This mode only allows fully transparent or fully opaque pixels. Harsh edges will be visible unless some form of screen-space antialiasing is enabled (see [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/screen_space_aa]). On the bright side, this mode doesn't suffer from transparency sorting issues when multiple transparent materials are overlapping. This mode is also known as [i]alpha testing[/i] or [i]1-bit transparency[/i].

values.name: ALPHA_CUT_HASH
values.summary: This mode draws cuts off all values below a spatially-deterministic threshold, the rest will remain opaque.

values.name: ALPHA_CUT_OPAQUE_PREPASS
values.summary: This mode draws fully opaque pixels in the depth prepass. This is slower than [constant ALPHA_CUT_DISABLED] or [constant ALPHA_CUT_DISCARD], but it allows displaying translucent areas and smooth edges while using proper sorting.

values.name: FLAG_DISABLE_DEPTH_TEST
values.summary: Disables the depth test, so this object is drawn on top of all others. However, objects drawn after it in the draw order may cover it.

values.name: FLAG_DOUBLE_SIDED
values.summary: If set, texture can be seen from the back as well. If not, the texture is invisible when looking at it from behind.

values.name: FLAG_FIXED_SIZE
values.summary: Label is scaled by depth so that it always appears the same size on screen.

values.name: FLAG_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DrawFlags] enum.

values.name: FLAG_SHADED
values.summary: If set, lights in the environment affect the sprite.

values.name: FLAG_TRANSPARENT
values.summary: If set, the texture's transparency and the opacity are used to make those parts of the sprite invisible.

constants.name: FLAG_TRANSPARENT
constants.summary: If set, the texture's transparency and the opacity are used to make those parts of the sprite invisible.

constants.name: FLAG_SHADED
constants.summary: If set, lights in the environment affect the sprite.

constants.name: FLAG_DISABLE_DEPTH_TEST
constants.summary: Disables the depth test, so this object is drawn on top of all others. However, objects drawn after it in the draw order may cover it.

constants.name: FLAG_FIXED_SIZE
constants.summary: Label is scaled by depth so that it always appears the same size on screen.

constants.name: FLAG_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DrawFlags] enum.

Harsh edges will be visible unless some form of screen-space antialiasing is enabled (see [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/screen_space_aa]). On the bright side, this mode doesn't suffer from transparency sorting issues when multiple transparent materials are overlapping. This mode is also known as [i]alpha testing[/i] or [i]1-bit transparency[/i].
